purpose: Routing catalog for matching designed HTML to Campaign Cart starter template families and commerce-ready surfaces. Keep matching cheap and deterministic; ask the user when confidence is not high.

doNotInferFrom: one shared include such as payment-methods.html

copyReviewPolicy.purpose: Market-sensitive starter copy must be confirmed against the target campaign instead of blindly carried forward or automatically removed.
appliesWhen: builder records market mode as country-specific or multi-market
reviewSurfaces: visible carrier, warehouse, and manufacturing claims

warnOnlyPatterns.label: US shipping promise
examples: free shipping on all US orders

copyReviewPolicy.agentRule: When a warn-only pattern appears in a country-specific or multi-market campaign, treat it as replace-or-confirm campaign copy. Preserve it only when the CampaignSpec, source design, or operator explicitly confirms the claim. Do not remove it automatically.

packages.purpose: Named Campaigns App package ref_ids used by checkout selectors, bumps, and display bindings.
packages.agentRule: Replace starter refs from the target CampaignSpec/API. Remove optional bump surfaces when the referenced prepurchase package is absent.

shipping_methods.purpose: Named Campaigns App shipping method ref_ids used by checkout selector cards.
shipping_methods.agentRule: Use only on checkout/cart selector surfaces. Do not carry starter refs between campaigns; remove shipping_method rows when the target campaign has no matching method.

bundles.purpose: Checkout bundle-card rows for package quantity tiers.
sourceOfTruth: Campaigns API package and shipping refs
bundles.agentRule: Prefer package_id plus quantity. Use explicit items_json only for genuinely custom item sets.

variant_slots.purpose: MV/configurable bundle slots for selector rows and later variant choice stages.
sourceOfTruth: Campaigns API package refs and variant config
variant_slots.agentRule: Keep selector ids aligned with data-next-bundle-slots-for. Preserve configurable bundle item shape unless the API/spec says the package is non-configurable.

order_bump.agentRule: Enable only when the target campaign has the referenced package or offer. Do not leave warranty/insurance starter modules pointed at missing package refs. On Apollo and Apollo MV checkouts, `selector_order_bump_variant` controls the product-card bump immediately below the bundle/MV selector (`check03` or `none`); `order_bump_variant` controls only the later `.order-bumps` form-section slot — do not treat `order_bump_variant` as all bumps on the page.

sourceOfTruth: Campaigns API package refs and vouchers/offers
upsell_offer.agentRule: Post-purchase upsells do not use shipping_methods. Package refs and vouchers must come from the upsell page contract.

sourceOfTruth: Campaigns API package refs and vouchers/offers
upsell_bundle_tiers.agentRule: Keep display tier and in-offer selector ids paired. Do not attach checkout shipping refs to upsell cards.

upsell_mv_tiers.purpose: Tiered post-purchase MV/configurable bundle upsell rows.
sourceOfTruth: Campaigns API configurable package refs and vouchers/offers
upsell_mv_tiers.agentRule: Pair with upsell_offer.package_id. Keep quantity tier ids aligned with upsells-mv.js and the hidden bundle selector; do not attach checkout shipping refs to MV upsells.

receipt_summary.purpose: Receipt order-item template ids and summary copy.
receipt_summary.agentRule: Preserve data-next-order-items templates and ids; change copy only through frontmatter when the family exposes it.

payment_flags.purpose: Visible payment method rows and express wallet flags.
payment_flags.agentRule: Payment config, CampaignSpec/API support, and visible rows must agree.

slot_personalization.purpose: MV slot-picker line-item personalization (SDK 0.4.27+). Adds a data-next-property input to each rendered bundle slot — on the checkout/select selector AND the post-purchase upsell offer — so units carry distinct text and become separate order lines. Same contract name across both surfaces.
sourceOfTruth: Template slot partials: mv-configurable-selector.html / mv-slot-stage.html (checkout/select) and upsell-mv-offer.html (upsell)
slot_personalization.agentRule: MV/slot-rendering families only — per-line customization requires a slot picker (eng-confirmed); not available on tier-swap families. Same `slot_personalization` contract on MV checkout/select and MV upsell. Shipped enabled on `apollo-mv-single-step` and `olympus-mv-single-step` (checkout.html + upsell-mv.html), disabled on `olympus-mv-two-step` (one live reference each). To apply on any other MV page, set slot_personalization.enabled:true in that page's frontmatter — the shared partial renders the field from the same contract (no markup copy). property_key becomes the order line-item attribute name. Fill every slot explicitly; do not rely on an order-wide default to fill some slots and override others (same-package overrides drop). When paired with order_personalization, keep property_keys distinct (per-slot overrides the page default on a shared key).

order_personalization.purpose: All-lines line-item personalization default (SDK 0.4.27+) via data-next-default-property. One value is applied to every line the active bundle/cart write or upsell accept sends; on a single non-slot upsell this means the one accepted line gets the value.
sourceOfTruth: Campaign personalization requirement (one shared value across all units or one value on a single non-slot upsell)
Template partial: personalization-field.html; offer partials may render the same order_personalization contract with data-next-default-property
order_personalization.agentRule: Reliable for 'same value on every unit' and for single non-variant / non-slot upsells where the accepted set is one line. The generic partial ships in every family's _includes but is NOT wired into generic pages; opt in with a campaign_include or an offer partial that accepts order_personalization. For per-line distinct slot values use slot_personalization (MV checkout/select/upsell) instead. When paired with a per-slot field on the same surface, use a DISTINCT property_key: the merge is { ...default, ...slot } so a per-slot value overrides the default on a shared key (MV checkout uses custom_text vs slot number; MV upsell example uses gift_message vs slot upsell_message).

campaignSpecFixturePolicy.purpose: Small CampaignSpec-shaped examples for agent contract reasoning. They are not live Campaigns App exports.
fixtureOnlyConventions: sdk_hints.template_family names the starter family for the fixture only.
sdk_hints.frontmatter shows the intended starter-template frontmatter mapping.
ref_id values are illustrative and must be replaced from the target Campaigns API.

olympus.description: Single-step checkout with tiered bundle cards and strong direct-response checkout framing.
strongSignals: olympus in spec, path, or export metadata
bundle selector include receives bundles frontmatter
side cart summary with Olympus card pricing rows

agentContract.templateRole: Classic single-step checkout family with tiered os-card bundle cards and optional pre-purchase bumps. Prefer `apollo` for new campaigns unless the design explicitly matches the Olympus os-card shape.

removeWhenUnsupported: bundles[].shipping_method when no tier-specific shipping exists
bump includes when prepurchase packages are absent
unsupported payment method rows
upsell pages not present in the funnel

agentNotes: Use this family when the design explicitly has the classic Olympus os-card tier shape. For new direct-response checkouts, default to `apollo` unless os-card styling is required.
Olympus bundle upsell pages use include-owned offer partials; replace upsell_offer and upsell_bundle_tiers from the target CampaignSpec/API.

checkout.description: Rendered Olympus checkout must adopt the starter-template shell, not a custom shell with only borrowed partials.

apollo.description: Single-step checkout with Apollo direct-response layout, include-driven bundle selector with interchangeable card styles, and optional checkout reveal.
strongSignals: apollo in spec, path, or export metadata
promo-banner and promo-timer partials (not web components)

agentContract.templateRole: Flagship single-step checkout: Apollo direct-response layout, tiered bundle cards via style dispatcher, selector-area check03 bump, optional checkout reveal, and form-section bumps.

starterTemplate: promoted checkout component CSS in next-core.css

removeWhenUnsupported: bundles[].shipping_method when no tier-specific shipping exists
selector_order_bump_variant when no selector-area bump package exists
bump includes when prepurchase packages are absent
unsupported payment method rows
upsell pages not present in the funnel

surfaces.name: tiered bundle selector with card style dispatcher
surfaces.note: product and tiles card styles show coupon extra savings via data-next-discounts="voucher" gated by cart.hasCoupon(). Styling lives in shared next-core.css — no per-page bundle-card CSS link.

surfaces.name: Apollo checkout layout blocks
surfaces.note: Checkout component CSS is in shared next-core.css. Checkout page styles: typically Swiper CDN only.

surfaces.name: checkout reveal
surfaces.note: initCheckoutReveal() in checkout-apollo.js. Hidden panel is off-screen (not display:none) so Spreedly card iframes mount at real size.

agentNotes: Use Apollo when the design has the Apollo checkout shell (trust bar, product heading, promo banner/timer, as-seen-in, reviews slider, guarantee) plus tiered bundle cards — not a generic Olympus clone.
Two bump slots: selector_order_bump_variant (check03 below selector) vs order_bump_variant (later form-section bumps). check03 config reads order_bump.check03.* frontmatter only.
Do not add per-page checkout component CSS files; promoted styles live in canonical next-core.css synced across all nine families.

checkout.description: Rendered Apollo checkout must adopt the starter-template shell, not a custom shell with only borrowed partials.

demeter.description: Single-step editorial/luxury checkout with spacious tier cards and open cart summary.
strongSignals: demeter in spec, path, or export metadata
luxury/editorial spacing with serif display type
demeter upsell offer includes with upsell_offer or upsell_bundle_tiers frontmatter

agentContract.templateRole: Editorial single-step checkout with tier cards and an open side summary.

removeWhenUnsupported: bundles[].shipping_method when no tier-specific shipping exists
order bump variants when prepurchase packages are absent

agentNotes: Demeter text such as shipping labels is copy, not proof; validate actual free/standard shipping against spec/API before launch.

checkout.description: Rendered Demeter checkout must adopt the starter-template shell, not a custom shell with only borrowed partials.

shop-single-step.description: Highly used shop-style single-step checkout with compact cart summary, receipt, and three bundle upsell variants.
strongSignals: shop-single-step in spec, path, or export metadata
shop checkout with checkout.html plus upsell-bundle-stepper/tier-pills/tier-cards pages
receipt order summary split into mobile and desktop order item templates
upsell bundle offers promoted to shop-single-step *_offer includes

agentContract.templateRole: Shop-style single-step checkout with compact cart summary plus promoted receipt and upsell includes.

removeWhenUnsupported: unsupported payment method rows
order bump when no bump package exists
upsell pages not present in the funnel

agentNotes: Shop-single-step is a real family, not a generic fallback. Preserve its summary/payment template roots.

checkout.description: Rendered shop single-step checkout must adopt the starter-template shell, not a custom shell with only borrowed partials.

olympus-mv-single-step.description: Olympus-style single-step checkout with configurable multi-variant bundle slots in the checkout page.

surfaces.name: MV variant-picker (alternative selector)
surfaces.note: Additive alternative to mv-configurable-selector: custom-JS slot rendering (variant-picker.js) with per-variant swatch thumbnails driven from one declared variant_picker.variants source, plus an offline render fixture. Supports per-tier data-next-shipping-id via variant_picker.tiers[].shipping_method/shipping_id. Demoed on the standalone variant-picker.html page; not wired into checkout.html by default.

agentNotes: Keep slot ids aligned across selector rows, bundle slots, and JS.

MV upsell pages now use upsells-mv.js for configurable-slot upsells and upsells.js for the standard bundle upsell chain; replace both upsell_offer and tier voucher/package refs from the target spec/API.

checkout.description: Rendered Olympus MV single-step checkout must adopt the starter-template shell, not a custom shell with only borrowed partials.

agentNotes: Hybrid of Olympus MV commerce surfaces and Apollo checkout layout. Pick this over olympus-mv-single-step when the design needs Apollo framing (trust bar, reviews slider, checkout reveal, etc.).
selector_layout grid|vertical on checkout_step. Two bump slots: selector_order_bump_variant vs order_bump_variant.

askUserPromptTemplate: I can map this design to more than one commerce template family: {candidates}. The ambiguous surfaces are {surfaces}. Which family should I use before wiring SDK components?

intentionalVariants.purpose: Shared includes that legitimately differ across families. These lineages are DELIBERATE design/structure differences, not drift — do NOT flag or auto-reconcile them in cross-family include-drift audits. Families omitted from an include's lineages do not ship that include.
intentionalVariants.regenerate: For each include, hash the body across families with the next_component/HTML-comment header and whitespace stripped; families with the same hash share a lineage.

lineages.note: MV/configurable bump: quantity sync via data-next-product-sync (product_id), so it sums across variant swaps (SDK 0.4.25+). See CLAUDE.md.
Shop bump layout for selector-less checkouts.
Shared unsynced product-card bump with optional PackageToggle line-item property field (SDK 0.4.27+).
Apollo trust-bar header (promoted checkout CSS in next-core).
Shop top-bar header (checkout-header--lg). See CLAUDE.md shop checkout top-bar notes.
Apollo + Olympus featured-product order summary (annotated reference).
Shop structural rewrite of cart-summary04 (188 lines vs olympus's 143).
